title: Error: channel "123" found, 0-3 supported: lost synchronization with multiplexed stream
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to set up a MySQL container on Apple M1 Pro.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Following command:

```
  podman run \
    --log-level=debug \
    -d \
    --rm \
    -v "/Users/pieterm/Work/mysql/data":/var/lib/mysql \
    -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD= \
    -e MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=1 \
    -p 3306:3306 \
    --name mysql \
    --platform linux/arm64 \
    mysql:8.0.28-oracle
```

2. The output is the ID of the container.
3. Running `podman ps` results in an empty table/list.

**Describe the results you received:**

Running `podman logs mysql` gives:

```
Error: channel "123" found, 0-3 supported: lost synchronization with multiplexed stream
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A running container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:** 

```

Version:      4.1.0

Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri May  6 18:16:38 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

Yes

comments.body: Does it happen every time? Can you try a different image?

I get this error on Linux (in remote mode inside a container) whenever the image isn't able to start for whatever reason. So isn't not a macOS only bug.

The only way to get the logs is do `podman ps -a`, find the failed container, and do a `podman logs` using that ID.

> whenever the image isn't able to start for whatever reason

What does this mean? Can you provide an exact reproducer.

I'll try and provide a reproducer later. 

I have encountered this problem this morning using `podman play kube` inside a container, with podman setup in remote mode. 

If for some reason one of the container in the pods fails to start (because of misconfiguration), running `podman pod logs -c container pod` shows this exact same error.

> Does it happen every time? Can you try a different image?

It happens every time. I tried with the official Fedora docker image but it gives the same result.

Update: I can make it work by omitting the `-v` argument, so without mounting.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@pierot @1player Is this still an issue?  Has it been fixedupstream?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I never heard back, I am closing.
What' the solution for that? I have the same problem podman on Mac

Getting the exact same issue with Mac on version 4.3.0.  Any solution?

During volume mount noticed that "Job for systemd-timesyncd.service failed." Not sure that it is related to the above issue
Please open a new issue with a reproducer instead of commenting on old ones.
